headline: China's AI darling DeepSeek previews new model
summary: DeepSeek, the Chinese artificial intelligence startup whose low-cost model stunned the world ‌last year, launched a preview of its ‌highly anticipated new model, the V4, on Friday.  The pro version of ​V4 outperforms other open-source models in world-knowledge benchmarks, trailing only Google's Gemini-Pro-3.1 which is a closed-source model, DeepSeek said.  Owned by China's High-Flyer Capital Management, DeepSeek is aiming to raise funds ​at a ​valuation exceeding $20 billion, according ​to a report by ‌The Information this month, which also said that tech giants Alibaba and Tencent were in discussions to take stakes.

content.title: China's AI darling DeepSeek previews new model
content.summary: DeepSeek, the Chinese artificial intelligence startup whose low-cost model stunned the world ‌last year, launched a preview of its ‌highly anticipated new model, the V4, on Friday.  The pro version of ​V4 outperforms other open-source models in world-knowledge benchmarks, trailing only Google's Gemini-Pro-3.1 which is a closed-source model, DeepSeek said.  Owned by China's High-Flyer Capital Management, DeepSeek is aiming to raise funds ​at a ​valuation exceeding $20 billion, according ​to a report by ‌The Information this month, which also said that tech giants Alibaba and Tencent were in discussions to take stakes.

headline: Oil extends gains, stocks sink as peace talk hopes fade
summary: Oil prices extended gains Friday and stocks struggled as investors worried about a lack of progress in ending the Middle East crisis, with Tehran keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed and the US maintaining a blockade of Iranian ports."So far, no meaningful progress has been made in negotiations, with both sides maintaining relatively firm positions, leaving the short-term outlook unclear.

headline: Markets Recover With Focus on Big Tech Earnings Over Iran. Stock Futures Rise.
summary: Stocks looked set to rise on Friday as solid tech earnings helped reassure investors who were questioning whether the market could keep its recent rally going.  The three major indexes all fell on Thursday as tensions flared up in the Strait of Hormuz and International Business Machines and ServiceNow both reported unspectacular earnings, sparking another selloff in software stocks.  Oil prices were barely moving on Friday as President Donald Trump said the cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon would extend another three weeks.

content.title: Markets Recover With Focus on Big Tech Earnings Over Iran. Stock Futures Rise.

Oil prices were barely moving on Friday as President Donald Trump said the cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon would extend another three weeks.

headline: China's AI darling DeepSeek previews new model adapted for Huawei chip technology
summary: DeepSeek, the Chinese artificial intelligence startup whose low-cost model stunned the world last year, on Friday launched a preview of its highly awaited new model adapted ‌for Huawei chip technology, underlining China's growing prowess in the sector.  The close collaboration with Huawei on the model, ‌the V4, contrasts with DeepSeek's past reliance on Nvidia's chips, though the startup did not disclose which processors it used to train its latest model.  The pro ​version of the new model outperforms other open-source models in world-knowledge benchmarks, trailing only Google's Gemini-Pro-3.1, which is a closed-source model, DeepSeek said.

content.title: China's AI darling DeepSeek previews new model adapted for Huawei chip technology
content.summary: DeepSeek, the Chinese artificial intelligence startup whose low-cost model stunned the world last year, on Friday launched a preview of its highly awaited new model adapted ‌for Huawei chip technology, underlining China's growing prowess in the sector.  The close collaboration with Huawei on the model, ‌the V4, contrasts with DeepSeek's past reliance on Nvidia's chips, though the startup did not disclose which processors it used to train its latest model.  The pro ​version of the new model outperforms other open-source models in world-knowledge benchmarks, trailing only Google's Gemini-Pro-3.1, which is a closed-source model, DeepSeek said.

headline: Analysis-At SpaceX, AI is burning the cash that Starlink earns
summary: Elon Musk is touting SpaceX as humanity's ticket to Mars.  The difference is in how the companies fund ‌the spending.  While Alphabet and Microsoft have deep operating cash flows, SpaceX is bankrolling its push with revenue from rockets and satellites, leaving it with a cash-burn profile ‌closer to a late-stage startup than a trillion-dollar incumbent.

content.title: Analysis-At SpaceX, AI is burning the cash that Starlink earns
content.summary: Elon Musk is touting SpaceX as humanity's ticket to Mars.  The difference is in how the companies fund ‌the spending.  While Alphabet and Microsoft have deep operating cash flows, SpaceX is bankrolling its push with revenue from rockets and satellites, leaving it with a cash-burn profile ‌closer to a late-stage startup than a trillion-dollar incumbent.

headline: Stocks Take a Breather After Record Run. Big Tech Earnings and Fed Meeting Loom Large.
summary: Investors will need to rest up ahead of next week’s bruising slate of corporate earnings, economic data, and central bank policy meetings.  The  took a breather on Thursday, falling around 30 points from its recent record high as global oil prices extended their weekly run of gains amid rising U.S.-Iran tensions and an unsteady truce in the broader Gulf region.  “Markets are entering the final day of the trading week in a cautious mood as U.S.-Iran tensions show no signs of easing while the Strait of Hormuz remains essentially closed,” said Deutsche Bank’s global head of macro research Jim Reid.

content.title: Stocks Take a Breather After Record Run. Big Tech Earnings and Fed Meeting Loom Large.
content.summary: Investors will need to rest up ahead of next week’s bruising slate of corporate earnings, economic data, and central bank policy meetings.  The  took a breather on Thursday, falling around 30 points from its recent record high as global oil prices extended their weekly run of gains amid rising U.S.-Iran tensions and an unsteady truce in the broader Gulf region.  “Markets are entering the final day of the trading week in a cautious mood as U.S.-Iran tensions show no signs of easing while the Strait of Hormuz remains essentially closed,” said Deutsche Bank’s global head of macro research Jim Reid.

headline: Oil extends gains, stocks drop as peace talk hopes fade
summary:
Oil prices extended gains Friday and stock markets largely declined as investors worried about a lack of progress in ending the Middle East crisis, with Tehran keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed and the US maintaining a blockade of Iranian ports.However on Friday, "stocks are down across the board, risk sentiment is off and oil prices are higher", noted Neil Wilson, investor strategist at Saxo UK.
"There's no real progress in ending the war between the US and Iran."

content.title: Oil extends gains, stocks drop as peace talk hopes fade
content.summary:
Oil prices extended gains Friday and stock markets largely declined as investors worried about a lack of progress in ending the Middle East crisis, with Tehran keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed and the US maintaining a blockade of Iranian ports.However on Friday, "stocks are down across the board, risk sentiment is off and oil prices are higher", noted Neil Wilson, investor strategist at Saxo UK.
"There's no real progress in ending the war between the US and Iran."

thumbnail.caption: Iran said it would not join talks to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while the United States maintained a blockade of the country's ports (-)

headline: DeepSeek previews new AI model adapted to run on Huawei chips
summary: DeepSeek, the Chinese startup whose low-cost AI model stunned the world last year, on Friday launched a preview of a highly awaited new model adapted for Huawei chip technology, underlining China's growing self-sufficiency in the sector.  The Pro version of the new ‌model outperforms other open-source models in world-knowledge benchmarks, trailing only Google's closed-source Gemini-Pro-3.1, DeepSeek said.  "This is a big deal for China's AI industry," said He Hui, director of semiconductor research at consultancy Omdia.

content.title: DeepSeek previews new AI model adapted to run on Huawei chips

The Pro version of the new ‌model outperforms other open-source models in world-knowledge benchmarks, trailing only Google's closed-source Gemini-Pro-3.1, DeepSeek said.  "This is a big deal for China's AI industry," said He Hui, director of semiconductor research at consultancy Omdia.

headline: Is IBM’s Q1 Beat A Classic Valuation Trap?

headline: Next Week Will Test the Stock Market’s Record-Setting Rally
summary: Investors will need to rest up ahead of next week’s bruising slate of corporate earnings, economic data, and central bank policy meetings.  The  took a breather on Thursday, falling around 30 points from its recent record high as global oil prices extended their weekly run of gains amid rising U.S.-Iran tensions and an unsteady truce in the broader Gulf region.  “Markets are entering the final day of the trading week in a cautious mood as U.S.-Iran tensions show no signs of easing while the Strait of Hormuz remains essentially closed,” said Deutsche Bank’s global head of macro research Jim Reid.

headline: Google plans to invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Google-parent Alphabet will invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic, Bloomberg News reported on Friday, as the tech ‌giant deepens its partnership with the startup which is also ‌its rival in the global AI race.  Google has committed $10 billion now in cash at a $350 ​billion valuation, the report said, adding that the company will invest a further $30 billion if Anthropic meets performance targets and support a major expansion of Anthropic's computing capacity.  Anthropic and Google did not immediately respond to Reuters' ‌requests for comment.

headline: DeepSeek unveils new AI model tailored for Huawei chips as China pushes for tech autonomy
summary: DeepSeek, the Chinese startup whose low-cost AI model stunned the world last year, launched on Friday a preview of a highly awaited new model adapted for Huawei chip technology, underlining China's growing autonomy in the sector.  The Pro version of the new model outperforms other open-source models in ‌world-knowledge benchmarks, trailing only Google's closed-source Gemini-Pro-3.1, DeepSeek said.  Huawei said ‌its chips were used in some of the V4's training process.

headline: Google to invest up to $40 billion in AI rival Anthropic
summary: Google-parent Alphabet will invest up to $40 billion in Anthropic, as the tech giant deepens its partnership with the artificial ‌intelligence startup that is also its rival in the global AI ‌race.  Anthropic said on Friday that Google has committed $10 billion now in cash at a valuation ​of $350 billion to help support a major expansion of its computing capacity, and will invest $30 billion more if the Claude maker meets performance targets.  The investment comes just days after e-commerce giant Amazon said it will invest up to $25 billion ‌in the startup, which has ⁠managed to stand out in the crowded AI industry by focusing its model training on coding.

Crypto miner Hut 8 (HUT) is reportedly tapping the bond market to raise money for a Google-backed (GOOG, GOOGL) data center.

content.title: Intel stock hits record high, Hut 8's Google-backed data center

Yahoo Finance Senior Producer John Hyland tracks Friday's top moving stocks and biggest market stories.

The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is on track for its fourth straight week of gains.

Intel (INTC) stock is skyrocketing after the company reported earnings, reaching a new all-time high.

Crypto miner Hut 8 (HUT) is reportedly tapping the bond market to raise money for a Google-backed (GOOG, GOOGL) data center.

headline: Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman, Mag 7 earnings, Fed meeting: What to Watch
summary: Asking for a Trend Host Josh Lipton previews several of the biggest stories to come next week, including the start of the trial between OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) co-founders Sam Altman and Elon Musk; earnings results from Magnificent Seven tech companies Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Meta Platforms (META), and Apple (AAPL); and the Federal Reserve's latest interest rate decision to come at its April FOMC meeting.

headline: Related Digital secures financing for $16 billion Oracle data center in Michigan
summary: Data center developer Related Digital said on Friday it has secured financing ‌for a $16 billion data center campus it ‌is building in Michigan for Oracle. The financing includes equity from ​Related Digital and funds affiliated with Blackstone, as well as fixed-rate, long-term debt financing anchored by PIMCO-managed funds and accounts, the company said. PIMCO bought about $10 ‌billion of the ⁠bonds that priced Friday, while other investors bought the remainder of the debt, Bloomberg ⁠News reported, citing people familiar.

content.title: Related Digital secures financing for $16 billion Oracle data center in Michigan

PIMCO bought about $10 ‌billion of the ⁠bonds that priced Friday, while other investors bought the remainder of the debt, Bloomberg ⁠News reported, citing people familiar.
